overview.short: The Lakshana Devi Temple in Bharmour is a post-Gupta era Hindu temple in Himachal Pradesh dedicated to Durga in her Mahishasura-mardini form.
overview.description: The Lakshana Devi Temple in Bharmour is a post-Gupta era Hindu temple in Himachal Pradesh dedicated to Durga in her Mahishasura-mardini form. It is dated to the second half of the 7th-century, and is in part one of the oldest surviving wooden temples in India. The temple is the oldest surviving structure of the former capital of Bharmour, also referred to as Bharmaur, Barmawar, Brahmor or Brahmapura in historic texts. Its roof and walls have been repaired over the centuries and it looks like a hut, but the Himachal Hindu community has preserved its intricately carved wooden entrance, interior

topPlaces.name: Chaurasi Temple Bharmour
topPlaces.description: Chaurasi Temple is a Hindu temple complex consisting of 84 different shrines, located in the center of Bharmour town of Chamba district in Himachal Pradesh, India. It holds immense religious importance because of temples built around 1400 years ago.

topPlaces.name: Bharmour
topPlaces.description: Bharmour is a village in the Indian state of Himachal Pradesh. Located in Chamba district, it is known for the Chaurasi Temple complex.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Lakshana Devi Temple, Bharmour?
faq.a: The best time to visit Lakshana Devi Temple, Bharmour is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Lakshana Devi Temple, Bharmour?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Kangra Airport (Gaggal), Dharamshala (40 km) and the nearest railway station is Jammu Tawi (160 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Lakshana Devi Temple, Bharmour?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Lakshana Devi Temple, Bharmour at a relaxed pace.
